name: Barbell Bench Press
steps: Lie flat on a bench, gripping the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and hold it straight over your chest with your arms locked.
Inhale and lower the barbell slowly to your mid-chest level.
Push the bar back up powerfully while exhaling, keeping your elbows slightly tucked.
mistakes: Bouncing the bar off your chest.
Flaring elbows out to a 90-degree angle.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
steps: Set an incline bench to around 30 to 45 degrees.
Sit back with a dumbbell in each hand, resting them on your thighs.
Press the dumbbells up to arm length over your upper chest.
Lower the weights under control until they are close to the sides of your chest.
Contract your pectorals and press the dumbbells back to the starting position.
mistakes: Using an incline angle that is too steep (shifts focus to shoulders).
Clicking dumbbells together at the top (loses tension).

name: Standing Cable Fly
steps: Set cable pulleys to chest height and select appropriate weights.
Grasp handles and step forward in a staggered stance, leaning slightly forward.
With a slight bend in your elbows, sweep your arms forward in a wide arc until hands meet.
Slowly reverse the movement back to the starting point, feeling a stretch in your chest.
mistakes: Pressing the weight instead of flying.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
steps: Rack a barbell at chest height. Grip with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar and hold it on your collarbone. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Press the bar straight up overhead, moving your head back slightly to let the bar pass.
Once the bar clears your face, bring your head back neutral and lock your arms out at the top.
Lower the bar slowly back to your collarbone.
mistakes: Not locking out at the top.
Using legs to push the bar (turns it into a push press).

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
steps: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing inward.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and lean your torso slightly forward.
Raise your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until they are parallel to the floor.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to your sides.
mistakes: Shrugging shoulders up while raising weights.
Swinging the body to lift the weight.

name: Cable Face Pull
steps: Attach a rope handle to a pulley set at upper chest height.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, step back to lift the weight stack, and stand tall.
Pull the rope towards your face, pulling the hands apart beside your ears while driving elbows back.
Squeeze the rear delts and upper back, then slowly return to the starting position.
mistakes: Dropping elbows (keep them high and wide).

name: Barbell Bicep Curl
steps: Stand upright holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your torso and pin them in place.
Curl the bar upward toward chest height by contracting your biceps.
Lower the bar slowly back to the starting position with full control.
mistakes: Swinging hips or back to cheat.
Not completing the full range of motion.

name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
steps: Stand holding dumbbells with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Keep your elbows close to your torso.
Curl the weights upward while maintaining the neutral hand position.
Squeeze at the top of the curl, then slowly lower the weights.
mistakes: Fast drop on the eccentric phase.

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
steps: Sit on an incline bench set to 45 degrees, letting arms hang straight down behind your torso.
Curl the dumbbells upward, keeping elbows locked in a backward position to emphasize the long head.
Squeeze your biceps at the top, then slowly lower the weights to full extension.
mistakes: Not letting arms fully extend at the bottom.

name: EZ-Bar Skull Crusher
steps: Lie flat on a bench holding an EZ-bar over your chest with straight arms.
Incline your upper arms back slightly (about 10 degrees) to maintain tension.
Bend at the elbows to lower the bar towards your forehead or slightly behind your head.
Extend your elbows to press the bar back up to the starting position.
mistakes: Flaring elbows out to the sides.
Moving the shoulder joint (upper arms should remain still).

name: Cable Rope Tricep Pushdown
steps: Attach a rope to a high pulley. Grasp handles and step back slightly.
Pin your elbows to your sides and lean forward slightly from the hips.
Push the rope down by extending your elbows, spreading the rope ends at the bottom.
Slowly return the rope back up to chest level, keeping elbows stationary.
mistakes: Allowing elbows to flare or move forward/backward.

name: Close-Grip Barbell Bench Press
steps: Lie on bench press setup, gripping the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart (not closer).
Unrack bar, lower it slowly to mid-chest while keeping elbows tucked close to your ribs.
Press the bar back up to extension, feeling the squeeze in the triceps.
mistakes: Gripping too closely (wrist and elbow strain).

name: Heavy Farmers Walk
steps: Pick up heavy dumbbells in each hand, standing tall with shoulders packed.
Walk forward in a straight line with slow, deliberate steps.
Maintain strong grip pressure and brace core throughout.
mistakes: Using light weights that don't challenge grip.

name: Barbell Reverse Wrist Curl
steps: Sit on a bench, resting forearms on thighs with wrists hanging over the knees.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip (palms down).
Extend wrists upward, lifting the back of your hands towards the ceiling.
Slowly lower the barbell to the starting wrist extension.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your legs straight and core engaged.
Raise your legs slowly by flexing your hips until they are parallel to the floor.
Control the descent back to the starting hang, preventing swinging.

name: Kneeling Cable Crunch
steps: Kneel in front of a cable machine with a rope attachment set high.
Hold rope handles next to your ears, bend forward slightly from the hips.
Contract your abs to flex your spine downward, drawing your elbows to your thighs.
Slowly return to start, feeling the resistance on your abs.
mistakes: Hinging at hips instead of flexing spine.

name: Pull-Up
steps: Hang from a bar with a pronated grip (palms facing away), hands wider than shoulders.
Depress and retract your scapulae (pull shoulders down and back).
Pull your chest up toward the bar by driving your elbows down to your ribs.
Clear the bar with your chin, then lower yourself under control to a dead hang.
mistakes: Not executing a full range of motion.
Shoulders shrugging up at the bottom.

name: Barbell Row
steps: Hold barbell with overhand grip. Hinge hips back until torso is 45 degrees to floor.
Pull the bar to your lower rib cage, driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades.
Lower the bar under control to arm extension.

name: Cable Lat Pulldown
steps: Sit at a lat pulldown machine, adjusting the thigh pads.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip, wider than shoulder-width.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar down to your collarbone, driving elbows down.
Control the return of the bar to the top extension.
mistakes: Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Pulling with hands instead of elbows.

name: Conventional Barbell Deadlift
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell over mid-foot. Grip outside your shins.
Flatten your spine, drop your hips, pull your chest up, and pull your shoulder blades down.
Brace your core, push through the floor, and stand up with the bar, keeping it close to your shins.
Lock out hips at the top. Lower the bar under control by pushing hips back and bending knees.
mistakes: Rounding the spine during the lift.
Hyperextending the back at the lockout.

name: Barbell Good Morning
steps: Set barbell on upper traps (same as squat). Stand tall.
Hinge hips back while keeping back flat and spine neutral, lowering torso to 60-90 degrees.
Drive hips forward to return to standing upright.
mistakes: Bending knees too much (turns into a squat).

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
steps: Sit on the floor with your upper back against the edge of a bench.
Place a padded barbell across your hips. Feet flat on the floor, shoulder-width.
Drive through your heels to extend your hips upward, squeeze glutes at the top.
Lower your hips back down under control.
mistakes: Hyper-extending lower back instead of tilting pelvis.
Feet placed too far forward (focuses on hamstrings).

name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
steps: Stand 2 feet in front of a bench. Place top of back foot on the bench.
Hold dumbbells in each hand. Lower hips until front thigh is parallel to floor.
Keep torso leaning slightly forward (targets glutes) and push back up through front heel.

name: Barbell Back Squat
steps: Rest barbell on upper traps, grip bar, feet shoulder-width, toes out 15 degrees.
Brace core, push hips back, bend knees, and lower until thighs are parallel to floor or deeper.
Keep chest upright and knees tracking in line with toes.
Drive back up through the midfoot to the standing starting position.
mistakes: Rounding lower back at the bottom ("butt wink").

name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge
steps: Stand upright holding dumbbells at your sides.
Take a large step forward with one leg, lowering hips until rear knee almost touches floor.
Front knee should be bent 90 degrees directly above the ankle.
Drive through the front heel to step forward and bring rear foot to meet front, or step directly into next lunge.
mistakes: Staggering balance (walking in a straight line instead of railroad tracks).

name: Sled Leg Press
steps: Sit on leg press machine, place feet on platform shoulder-width.
Lower platform slowly towards chest by bending knees 90 degrees.
Press platform away to arm extension, keeping knees slightly unlocked at top.
mistakes: Lowering too deep, causing lower back to lift off pad.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell at hip height.
Push your hips back as far as possible, softening knees slightly.
Lower the bar down your thighs, keeping your back completely flat.
When you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings, reverse the movement by pushing hips forward.
mistakes: Squatting down instead of hinging hips back.
Letting the bar drift away from legs.

name: Seated Leg Curl Machine
steps: Sit in machine, thighs locked under pads, heels over rollers.
Contract hamstrings to pull rollers downwards and backwards.
Pause at maximum bend, slowly release to full extension.

name: Standing Dumbbell Calf Raise
steps: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in one hand, other hand on a wall for balance.
Place the ball of one foot on a raised step, heel hanging off the edge.
Lower your heel as far down as possible to feel a deep stretch.
Raise up onto your toes as high as possible, squeezing the calf.
Lower back down slowly.
mistakes: Not using a full range of motion.

name: Seated Calf Raise Machine
steps: Sit in machine, platform under ball of feet, thighs under pad.
Lower heels as far as possible to stretch soleus muscle.
Press through toes to lift weight stack high, squeeze calves, lower under control.
